items.academy ball: The academy’s standard-issue ball in a regulation size. Lots of students believe that if you kick it right on the academy emblem, it’ll fly in a straight line.
items.academy bottle: A bottle emblazoned with the academy’s emblem. It’s light, sturdy, and vacuum insulated, so it’ll keep your hots hot and your colds cold.
items.academy cup: The academy’s standard-issue cup for picnicking. The careful design of its mouth makes it popular both inside and outside the academy.
items.academy tablecloth: The academy’s standard-issue tablecloth. It’s made with the latest fiber-processing technology and is so tough that not even a Razor Claw can tear it.
items.aipom hair: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.alomomola mucus: Alomomola Mucus is a Pokémon material dropped by the Alomomola family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.apple: This very sweet ingredient is sliced thin to make it easy to add into a sandwich.
items.applin juice: Applin Juice is a Pokémon material dropped by the Applin family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.arrokuda scales: Arrokuda Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Arrokuda family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.avocado: An ingredient with sweet notes—though its flavor is very mild. The avocado’s richness, however, makes it satisfying to eat.
items.axew scales: Axew Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Axew family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.azurill fur: Azurill Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Azurill family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.b&w grass tablecloth: A tablecloth with a fun, trendy black-and-white design featuring Pokémon that seem poised to leap from the tall grass at any moment.
items.bacon: This very salty ingredient has been cooked just shy of charred, making it burst with fragrance and tasty flavor.
items.bagon scales: Bagon Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bagon family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.baguette: A long loaf sliced into top and bottom halves. Its aroma of wheat and its straightforward, salty flavor make it pair well with all kinds of sandwich ingredients.
items.banana: A very sweet ingredient. This go-to fruit is sweet and rich, and it’s beloved by many Pokémon.
items.barboach slime: Barboach Slime is a Pokémon material dropped by the Barboach family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.barred cup: A cup that’s as good for using at home as on a picnic. It’s light, sturdy, and stacks well, so it’s a cinch to pack up and carry around.
items.basculin fang: Basculin Fang is a Pokémon material dropped by the Basculin family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.basil: A very bitter ingredient that’s quite versatile, as herbs go. It’s fragrant and goes well with tomatoes.
items.battle tablecloth: A garish, eye-catching tablecloth. Putting this on your picnic table will inspire any person or Pokémon who eats there to do their best.
items.bellsprout vine: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.bergmite ice: Bergmite Ice is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bergmite family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.big bamboo shoot: A large and rare bamboo shoot. It’s extremely popular with a certain class of gourmands.
items.bitter herba mystica: A legendary condiment with a deeply bitter taste. It’s said to dramatically bolster a body’s immune defenses, though that hasn’t been proven.
items.blank plate: A stone tablet imbued with the essence of normalcy. When used on a certain Pokémon, it allows that Pokémon to gain the power of the Normal type.
items.blue bottle: A go-to bottle widely used for drinks at picnics. Its simple design and portability make it popular among all sorts of people.
items.blue cup: A cup sold with the Blue Bottle. The two don’t stack, so they’re tough to pack—but they look good, so they’re popular with those who like to post picnic photos.
items.blue dish: A dish for picnics. Comes in plenty of colors to choose from based on the food or the mood. The blue variety is a consistent strong seller.
items.blue poké ball pick: A sandwich pick with a simple blue Poké Ball design. Often used by Trainers who picked a Water-type Pokémon for their first partner.
items.blue tablecloth: A simple blue tablecloth. It’s smooth and pleasant to the touch, and dishes can be placed on it with hardly a sound.
items.blue-flag pick: This pick flies a blue flag that lends an air of adventure. The flag itself is made of card stock, so it’ll fly proudly even with no wind.
items.blue-sky flower pick: A flower pick designed to evoke a blue sky. It’s part of a line of “sky-flower” picks. Using a few is guaranteed to boost that special picnic spirit.
items.bombirdier feather: Bombirdier Feather is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bombirdier family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.bonsly tears: Bonsly Tears is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bonsly family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.bounsweet sweat: Bounsweet Sweat is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bounsweet family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.bramblin twig: Bramblin Twig is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bramblin family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.bronze bottle: A popular option among hikers. It’s made with titanium, so it doesn’t rust even if it’s used to hold drinks with high sodium content.
items.bronze cup: A popular option among hikers. It’s made with titanium, so it doesn’t rust even if it’s used to hold drinks with high sodium content.
items.bronzor fragment: Bronzor Fragment is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bronzor family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.bruxish tooth: Bruxish Tooth is a Pokémon material dropped by the Bruxish family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.bug tera shard: Bug Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Bug in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Bug Tera Shards.
items.buizel fur: Buizel Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Buizel family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.butter: A condiment that unites salty and sweet and also packs a richness that makes bread a delight to eat. The finest butter products are made of 100 percent Moomoo Milk.
items.cacnea needle: Cacnea Needle is a Pokémon material dropped by the Cacnea family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.capsakid seed: Capsakid Seed is a Pokémon material dropped by the Capsakid family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.carbink jewel: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.cetoddle grease: Cetoddle Grease is a Pokémon material dropped by the Cetoddle family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.charcadet soot: Charcadet Soot is a Pokémon material dropped by the Charcadet family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.cheese: A very salty ingredient. This thinly sliced cheese is perfect for adding to a sandwich.
items.cherry tomatoes: A very tart ingredient. Some varieties are also very sweet, and these are prized above all others by the people of Paldea.
items.chewtle claw: Chewtle Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Chewtle family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.chili sauce: A condiment with a very spicy kick. It’s thanks to Scovillain that this sauce gets its signature mouth-tingling burn.
items.chingling fragment: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.chorizo: A very salty and spicy ingredient. This type of sausage has particularly robust salty flavor, and the spicier varieties are extra popular.
items.clauncher claw: Clauncher Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Clauncher family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.cleffa fur: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.combee honey: Combee Honey is a Pokémon material dropped by the Combee family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.corphish shell: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.crabrawler shell: Crabrawler Shell is a Pokémon material dropped by the Crabrawler family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.cramorant down: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.cream cheese: A condiment offering very sweet and very tart flavor. It goes well with more ingredients than you’d expect, making it a convenient go-to.
items.croagunk poison: Croagunk Poison is a Pokémon material dropped by the Croagunk family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.cryogonal ice: Cryogonal Ice is a Pokémon material dropped by the Cryogonal family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.crystal cluster: A cluster of the crystals found within the Crystal Pool. They sparkle brilliantly and are apparently necessary to repair a certain mask.
items.cubchoo fur: Cubchoo Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Cubchoo family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.cucumber: A tart and bitter ingredient. Using it together with rich-tasting ingredients in a sandwich will really make the most of a cucumber’s fresh-veggie aroma.
items.cufant tarnish: Cufant Tarnish is a Pokémon material dropped by the Cufant family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.curry powder: A very spicy condiment. Just watch out—use too much of this potent seasoning, and it’ll cancel out the other flavors.
items.cutiefly powder: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.cyber ball: A ball themed after hardcore gaming PCs. It may not light up like its inspiration, but it still stands out.
items.cyclizar scales: Cyclizar Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Cyclizar family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.dark tera shard: Dark Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Dark in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Dark Tera Shards.
items.dedenne fur: Dedenne Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Dedenne family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.deerling hair: Deerling Hair is a Pokémon material dropped by the Deerling family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.deino scales: Deino Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Deino family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.delibird parcel: Delibird Parcel is a Pokémon material dropped by the Delibird family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.diamond bottle: A diamond-patterned bottle that’s small, light, and even comes with a cup. The detachable strap makes it handy for walking around with.
items.diamond pattern cup: A cup that’s as good for using at home as on a picnic. It’s light, sturdy, and stacks well, so it’s a cinch to pack up and carry around.
items.diamond tablecloth: A tablecloth with a fashionable diamond pattern. It’s great at repelling water and mold, and stains and smudges wipe right off.
items.diglett dirt: Diglett Dirt is a Pokémon material dropped by the Diglett family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

items.ducklett feather: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.dunsparce scales: Dunsparce Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Dunsparce family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.duskull fragment: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.eevee cup: A cup for big-time Pokémon fans. Kids will happily drink out of it, making it a boon to parents.
items.eevee fur: Eevee Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Eevee family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.egg: An ingredient with a touch of saltiness. It’s full of nutrients and can be enjoyed with all sorts of seasonings.
items.eiscue down: Eiscue Down is a Pokémon material dropped by the Eiscue family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.ekans fang: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.electric tera shard: Electric Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Electric in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Electric Tera Shards.
items.exercise ball: A big ball designed for exercising. It can work as a seat if you get tired, too.
items.fairy tera shard: Fairy Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Fairy in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Fairy Tera Shards.
items.falinks sweat: Falinks Sweat is a Pokémon material dropped by the Falinks family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

items.fire pattern cup: A cup that’s as good for using at home as on a picnic. It’s light, sturdy, and stacks well, so it’s a cinch to pack up and carry around.
items.fire tera shard: Fire Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Fire in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Fire Tera Shards.
items.flabébé pollen: Flabébé Pollen is a Pokémon material dropped by the Flabébé family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

items.fletchling feather: Fletchling Feather is a Pokémon material dropped by the Fletchling family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.flittle down: Flittle Down is a Pokémon material dropped by the Flittle family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.flower pattern cup: A cup for kids to use at picnics. It’s made of light but strong material. It’s stackable, which makes it handy for carrying around.
items.flying tera shard: Flying Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Flying in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Flying Tera Shards.
items.fomantis leaf: Fomantis Leaf is a Pokémon material dropped by the Fomantis family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.foongus spores: Foongus Spores is a Pokémon material dropped by the Foongus family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.fried fillet: When used in a sandwich, this very salty and bitter ingredient is at its best paired with sour seasonings.
items.frigibax scales: Frigibax Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Frigibax family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.gastly gas: Gastly Gas is a Pokémon material dropped by the Gastly family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.geodude fragment: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.ghost tera shard: Ghost Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Ghost in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Ghost Tera Shards.
items.gible scales: Gible Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Gible family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.gimmighoul coin: Gimmighoul Coin is a Pokémon material dropped by Gimmighoul when they are captured or defeated in battle. Collecting 999 Coins allows Gimmighoul to evolve into Gholdengo. The bag limit is 999 Coins, however any extra are found by a man in Medali. Once Gimmighoul has been evolved those extra coins can be retrieved from the man.
items.girafarig fur: Girafarig Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Girafarig family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.gligar fang: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.glimmering charm: Having one of these mysterious glimmering charms increases the number of Tera Shards you receive from Tera Raid Battles.
items.glimmet crystal: Glimmet Crystal is a Pokémon material dropped by the Glimmet family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.gold bottle: A popular option among hikers. It’s made with titanium, so it doesn’t rust even if it’s used to hold drinks with high sodium content.
items.gold cup: A popular option among hikers. It’s made with titanium, so it doesn’t rust even if it’s used to hold drinks with high sodium content.
items.gold pick: A golden pick that oozes class. Using it on a sandwich lends an extravagant air that’ll make you want to eat up, even if you don’t love the fillings.
items.goomy goo: Goomy Goo is a Pokémon material dropped by the Goomy family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.gothita eyelash: Gothita Eyelash is a Pokémon material dropped by the Gothita family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.grass tera shard: Grass Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Grass in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Grass Tera Shards.
items.greavard wax: Greavard Wax is a Pokémon material dropped by the Greavard family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.green bell pepper: An ingredient with some bitterness. When sliced, it adds a nice accent to a sandwich.
items.green dish: A dish for picnics. Comes in plenty of colors to choose from based on the food or the mood. The green variety is quite popular.
items.green poké ball pick: A sandwich pick with a simple green Poké Ball design. Often used by Trainers who picked a Grass-type Pokémon for their first partner.
items.grimer toxin: Grimer Toxin is a Pokémon material dropped by the Grimer family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.ground tera shard: Ground Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Ground in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Ground Tera Shards.
items.growlithe fur: Growlithe Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Growlithe family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.grubbin thread: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.gulpin mucus: Gulpin Mucus is a Pokémon material dropped by the Gulpin family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.ham: A very salty ingredient—and that very saltiness is what makes it such a nice companion to veggies.
items.hamburger: This very salty ingredient is the thing to add if you want to make your sandwich truly filling.
items.happiny dust: Happiny Dust is a Pokémon material dropped by the Happiny family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.hatenna dust: Hatenna Dust is a Pokémon material dropped by the Hatenna family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.hawlucha down: Hawlucha Down is a Pokémon material dropped by the Hawlucha family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.heracross claw: Heracross Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Heracross family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.herbed sausage: A very salty and bitter ingredient. The balance between the potent saltiness and the herbs’ bitterness make this sausage a pleasure.
items.heroic sword pick: A pick made to be the coolest. Once you’ve eaten, you can pretend you’re a hero of legend. Souvenir shops at tourist spots sell these for some reason.
items.hippopotas sand: Hippopotas Sand is a Pokémon material dropped by the Hippopotas family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.hoothoot feather: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.hoppip leaf: Hoppip Leaf is a Pokémon material dropped by the Hoppip family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.horseradish: A very spicy condiment. Its distinctive sharp sizzle in your sinuses makes it a good match for rich fillings.
items.houndour fang: Houndour Fang is a Pokémon material dropped by the Houndour family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.ice tera shard: Ice Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Ice in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Ice Tera Shards.
items.igglybuff fluff: Igglybuff Fluff is a Pokémon material dropped by the Igglybuff family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.illumise fluid: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.impidimp hair: Impidimp Hair is a Pokémon material dropped by the Impidimp family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.indeedee fur: Indeedee Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Indeedee family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.jalapeño: A very spicy ingredient—so spicy as to leave no room for ambivalence about it. You either like it or you don’t.
items.jam: A condiment that’s very sweet and likewise very sour. Take care when adding it to sandwiches, as the sweet side can overwhelm.
items.jangmo-o scales: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.ketchup: A condiment with very salty and tart flavor. While a number of other ingredients go into its signature flavor, the noble tomato is its heart and soul.
items.kiwi: A very tart ingredient that has a light touch of sweetness as well. It goes well with rich ingredients.
items.klawf claw: Klawf Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Klawf family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.klawf stick: A very sweet and salty ingredient. It’s a nutritious processed food made with a concentrate derived from shed Klawf shells.
items.klefki key: Klefki Key is a Pokémon material dropped by the Klefki family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.koffing gas: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.kofu’s wallet: A wallet that was left behind by Kofu, the Gym Leader at the Cascarrafa Gym. You’re to deliver it to him at the market in Porto Marinada.
items.komala claw: Komala Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Komala family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.koraidon’s poké ball: A Poké Ball that can hold the mysterious Pokémon known as Koraidon. A boy named Arven gave it to you.
items.kricketot shell: Kricketot Shell is a Pokémon material dropped by the Kricketot family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.larvesta fuzz: Larvesta Fuzz is a Pokémon material dropped by the Larvesta family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.larvitar claw: Larvitar Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Larvitar family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.leader’s crest: A shard of what appears to be an old blade of some sort. It is held only by Bisharp that head up a group of Pawniard.
items.leafy tablecloth: A botanically themed tablecloth featuring Grass-type Pokémon. Can you find the Pokémon that doesn’t quite fit in?
items.lechonk hair: Lechonk Hair is a Pokémon material dropped by the Lechonk family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.legend plate: A stone tablet imbued with the essence of all creation. When used on a certain Pokémon, it allows that Pokémon to gain the power of every type there is.
items.lettuce: This bitter-tasting ingredient is simple and straightforward—just add it to all kinds of other ingredients for a delightfully crunchy time.
items.lilac tablecloth: A light purple tablecloth. It’s smooth and pleasant to the touch, and dishes can be placed on it with hardly a sound.
items.litleo tuft: Litleo Tuft is a Pokémon material dropped by the Litleo family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.litwick soot: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.lotad leaf: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.luvdisc scales: Luvdisc Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Luvdisc family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.magical heart pick: A pick shaped after a magic wand. Perhaps the real magic is the food maker’s wish for a delicious dish.
items.magical star pick: A pick shaped after a magic wand. Perhaps the real magic is the food maker’s wish for a delicious dish.
items.magikarp scales: Magikarp Scales is a Pokémon material dropped by the Magikarp family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.magnemite screw: Magnemite Screw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Magnemite family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.makuhita sweat: Makuhita Sweat is a Pokémon material dropped by the Makuhita family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.mankey fur: Mankey Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Mankey family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.mareanie spike: Mareanie Spike is a Pokémon material dropped by the Mareanie family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.mareep wool: Mareep Wool is a Pokémon material dropped by the Mareep family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.marill ball: A Marill-themed ball. It’s soft and springy. Make sure you haven’t mistaken a real Marill for this ball before throwing.
items.marmalade: A condiment with sour and bitter notes. It’s surprisingly useful, as it makes a nice counterpoint to oilier foods.
items.maschiff fang: Maschiff Fang is a Pokémon material dropped by the Maschiff family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.mayonnaise: A condiment that packs a very tart punch yet pairs well with various ingredients.
items.meditite sweat: Meditite Sweat is a Pokémon material dropped by the Meditite family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.meowth fur: Meowth Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Meowth family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.mienfoo claw: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.mimikyu scrap: Mimikyu Scrap is a Pokémon material dropped by the Mimikyu family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.mint tablecloth: A light mint-colored tablecloth. It’s smooth and pleasant to the touch, and dishes can be placed on it with hardly a sound.
items.miraidon’s poké ball: A Poké Ball that can hold the mysterious Pokémon known as Miraidon. A boy named Arven gave it to you.
items.misdreavus tears: Misdreavus Tears is a Pokémon material dropped by the Misdreavus family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.monstrous tablecloth: A tablecloth designed by the Pewter Museum of Science. The academic air of the pattern is a big hit with kids and adults alike.
items.morpeko snack: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.mudbray mud: Mudbray Mud is a Pokémon material dropped by the Mudbray family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.munchlax fang: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.murkrow bauble: Murkrow Bauble is a Pokémon material dropped by the Murkrow family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.mustard: A very spicy condiment that’s indispensable when bread is on the table. It pairs tremendously with ketchup.
items.nacli salt: Nacli Salt is a Pokémon material dropped by the Nacli family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.noibat fur: Noibat Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Noibat family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.noodles: A nice salty ingredient. It’s unclear how well these boiled noodles will serve as a sandwich filling.
items.normal tera shard: Normal Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Normal in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Normal Tera Shards.
items.nosepass fragment: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.numel lava: Numel Lava is a Pokémon material dropped by the Numel family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.nymble claw: Nymble Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Nymble family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.olive oil: A condiment offering faint notes of sour and bitter flavors. The most prized varieties are the ones Smoliv love the smell of—such oils’ quality is assured.
items.onion: This spicy veggie is indispensable to many sandwiches. It goes well with rich ingredients.
items.orange dish: A dish for picnics. Comes in plenty of colors to choose from based on the food or the mood. The orange variety is the most popular of all.
items.oranguru fur: Oranguru Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Oranguru family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.oricorio feather: Oricorio Feather is a Pokémon material dropped by the Oricorio family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.origin ball: A singular and irreplicable Poké Ball that can be used to catch the frenzied Pokémon raging at the Temple of Sinnoh.
items.orthworm tarnish: Orthworm Tarnish is a Pokémon material dropped by the Orthworm family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.pachirisu fur: Pachirisu Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Pachirisu family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.parasol pick: Some complain that the parasol part of this pick is hard to hold on to, so your fingers get messy. Still, it’s fun to twirl this all around after you’ve eaten.
items.party sparkler pick: This pick features a small sparkler that goes off automatically. It’s used at parties or for guests of honor. It leaves an oddly appealing aroma once it’s burned out.
items.passimian fur: Passimian Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Passimian family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.pawmi fur: Pawmi Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Pawmi family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.pawniard blade: Pawniard Blade is a Pokémon material dropped by the Pawniard family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.peach tablecloth: A light peach-colored tablecloth. It’s smooth and pleasant to the touch, and dishes can be placed on it with hardly a sound.
items.peanut butter: A very sweet condiment. People in Paldea seem to favor the sweet varieties over the unsweetened.
items.pepper: A seasoning packing very spicy flavor. It goes nicely with oilier foods and works especially well when you add just a pinch for a hint of flavor.
items.petilil leaf: Petilil Leaf is a Pokémon material dropped by the Petilil family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.phanpy nail: Phanpy Nail is a Pokémon material dropped by the Phanpy family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.phantump twig: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.pichu fur: Pichu Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Pichu family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.pickle: This very sour ingredient is made by pickling veggies in a flavorful brine made from spices and vinegar.
items.picnic set: A roomy basket packed with all sorts of things for use when enjoying picnicking with your Pokémon.
items.pika-pika pick: A Pick-achu, as it were, sporting Pikachu’s usual expression. It’s easy to grab by the ears, which is part of why it’s so popular.
items.pikachu cup: A cup for big-time Pokémon fans. Kids will happily drink out of it, making it a boon to parents.
items.pincurchin spines: Pincurchin Spines is a Pokémon material dropped by the Pincurchin family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.pineapple: This very tart ingredient is a fruit of southern lands, and its color and texture are a thrill. It’s been cut into chunks to be easily manageable.
items.pineco husk: Pineco Husk is a Pokémon material dropped by the Pineco family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.pink bottle: A go-to bottle widely used for drinks at picnics. Its simple design and portability make it popular among all sorts of people.
items.pink cup: A cup sold with the Pink Bottle. The two don’t stack, so they’re tough to pack—but they look good, so they’re popular with those who like to post picnic photos.
items.pink tablecloth: A simple pink tablecloth. It’s smooth and pleasant to the touch, and dishes can be placed on it with hardly a sound.
items.plaid tablecloth (b): A sturdy tablecloth made of thick fabric. The gentle blue plaid gives a table a crisp look.
items.plaid tablecloth (r): A sturdy tablecloth made of thick fabric. The quietly composed red plaid gives the table some flair.
items.plaid tablecloth (y): A sturdy tablecloth made of thick fabric. The cheery yellow plaid brightens up a table.
items.poison tera shard: Poison Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Poison in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Poison Tera Shards.
items.poliwag slime: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.polka-dot bottle: A polka-dot-patterned bottle that’s small, light, and even comes with a cup. The detachable strap makes it handy for walking around with.
items.polka-dot cup: A cup for kids to use at picnics. It’s made of light but strong material. It’s stackable, which makes it handy for carrying around.
items.polka-dot tablecloth: A tablecloth with a fashionable polka-dot pattern. It’s great at repelling water and mold, and stains and smudges wipe right off.
items.poltchageist powder: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.poochyena fang: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.potato salad: This very tart ingredient—a soft salad that’s all about potatoes—pairs tremendously well with bread.
items.potato tortilla: A very salty ingredient and a popular dish in Paldea. It’s undeniably filling.
items.prosciutto: This ingredient is very salty like regular ham, but the difference is prosciutto is not cooked after it is cured, giving it a fun freshness.
items.psychic tera shard: Psychic Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Psychic in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Psychic Tera Shards.
items.psyduck down: Psyduck Down is a Pokémon material dropped by the Psyduck family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.qwilfish spines: Qwilfish Spines is a Pokémon material dropped by the Qwilfish family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.ralts dust: Ralts Dust is a Pokémon material dropped by the Ralts family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.red bell pepper: An ingredient with some bitterness—but its bitter notes and overall flavor are pleasantly mild compared to its green cousin.
items.red dish: A dish for picnics. Comes in plenty of colors to choose from based on the food or the mood. The red variety never fails to sell well.
items.red onion: An ingredient with some sweetness to it. Its spiciness and fragrance are milder than those of other onions, making it a pleasant bite.
items.red poké ball pick: A sandwich pick with a simple red Poké Ball design. Often used by Trainers who picked a Fire-type Pokémon for their first partner.
items.red-flag pick: This pick flies a red flag that lends an air of adventure. The flag itself is made of card stock, so it’ll fly proudly even with no wind.
items.rellor mud: Rellor Mud is a Pokémon material dropped by the Rellor family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.rice: An ingredient with a touch of sweetness. It’s unclear how well this cooked rice will serve as a sandwich filling.
items.riolu fur: Riolu Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Riolu family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.rock tera shard: Rock Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Rock in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Rock Tera Shards.
items.rockruff rock: Rockruff Rock is a Pokémon material dropped by the Rockruff family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.rolycoly coal: Rolycoly Coal is a Pokémon material dropped by the Rolycoly family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.rookidee feather: Rookidee Feather is a Pokémon material dropped by the Rookidee family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.roto stick: A stick to rest a Rotom Phone on. It allows you to take selfies from a little bit farther away than you could on your own.
items.rotom phone: The latest model of smartphone. A Pokémon called Rotom lives within it, and it can be used to run all sorts of handy apps.
items.rotom sparks: Rotom Sparks is a Pokémon material dropped by the Rotom family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.rufflet feather: Rufflet Feather is a Pokémon material dropped by the Rufflet family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.sableye gem: Sableye Gem is a Pokémon material dropped by the Sableye family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.salandit gas: Salandit Gas is a Pokémon material dropped by the Salandit family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.salt: A very salty seasoning, unsurprisingly. People quite like the grains of salt that can be gathered from the footprints of particularly jagged Naclstack.
items.salty herba mystica: One of the condiments spoken of as legends. Its mellow saltiness is said to be tremendously effective when it comes to promoting health.
items.sandile claw: Sandile Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Sandile family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.sandshrew claw: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.sandwich: A dish made by sandwiching vegetables, meat, or other ingredients between two pieces of bread. It makes a fantastic meal when out on a picnic.
items.sandygast sand: Sandygast Sand is a Pokémon material dropped by the Sandygast family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.scarlet book: A record of the expedition that the author, Heath, went on within the Great Crater of Paldea. The book has the name “Sada” written on it in clumsy handwriting.
items.scatterbug powder: Scatterbug Powder is a Pokémon material dropped by the Scatterbug family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.scroll of darkness: A peculiar scroll that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. Written upon it are the true secrets of the path of darkness.
items.scroll of waters: A peculiar scroll that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. Written upon it are the true secrets of the path of water.
items.scyther claw: Scyther Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Scyther family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

items.silver bottle: A popular option among hikers. It’s made with titanium, so it doesn’t rust even if it’s used to hold drinks with high sodium content.
items.silver cup: A popular option among hikers. It’s made with titanium, so it doesn’t rust even if it’s used to hold drinks with high sodium content.
items.silver pick: A silver pick that oozes class. Using it on a sandwich lends an extravagant air that’ll make you want to eat up, even if you don’t love the fillings.
items.sinistea chip: Sinistea Chip is a Pokémon material dropped by the Sinistea family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

items.slowpoke cup: A cup for big-time Pokémon fans. Kids will happily drink out of it, making it a boon to parents.
items.slugma lava: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.smiling vee pick: A pick designed after Eevee, sporting the Pokémon’s smiling face. It’s easy to grab by the ears, which is part of why it’s so popular.
items.smoked fillet: A very salty and bitter ingredient with that unmistakable smoky flavor. It pairs exceptionally well with veggies.
items.smoliv oil: Smoliv Oil is a Pokémon material dropped by the Smoliv family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.sneasel claw: Sneasel Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Sneasel family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

items.sour herba mystica: This surpassingly sour condiment is said to be super effective in helping the eater recover from exhaustion. Only a scant few people know it exists.
items.spicy herba mystica: The legendary condiment said to have the mightiest of all spicy flavors. It’s said that a single bite will kick the metabolism into high gear immediately.
items.spinarak thread: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.

items.steel bottle (b): A go-to bottle sometimes used while camping. The stainless steel keeps the temperature inside steady, making it great for keeping drinks hot or cold.
items.steel bottle (r): A go-to bottle sometimes used while camping. The stainless steel keeps the temperature inside steady, making it great for keeping drinks hot or cold.
items.steel bottle (y): A go-to bottle sometimes used while camping. The stainless steel keeps the temperature inside steady, making it great for keeping drinks hot or cold.

items.stonjourner stone: Stonjourner Stone is a Pokémon material dropped by the Stonjourner family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.strawberry: A very tart and sweet ingredient, popular for its ability to make a sandwich visually striking.
items.striped bottle: A striped bottle that’s small, light, and even comes with a cup. The detachable strap makes it handy for walking around with.
items.striped cup: A cup for kids to use at picnics. It’s made of light but strong material. It’s stackable, which makes it handy for carrying around.

items.sunset flower pick: A flower pick designed to evoke a blazing sunset. It’s part of a line of “sky-flower” picks. Using a few is guaranteed to boost that special picnic spirit.
items.surskit syrup: Surskit Syrup is a Pokémon material dropped by the Surskit family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.swablu fluff: Swablu Fluff is a Pokémon material dropped by the Swablu family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.sweet herba mystica: A legendary condiment told of only in books. Word has it that one taste of its sweet flavor stimulates the digestive system and cures a lack of appetite.
items.swinub hair: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.tadbulb mucus: Tadbulb Mucus is a Pokémon material dropped by the Tadbulb family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

items.teal mask: A teal mask patterned after the face of an ogre. Ogerpon dropped it while fleeing up the stairs after visiting the Festival of Masks.
items.teal style card: Having one of these cards increases the selection available to you in boutiques and hair salons.
items.teddiursa claw: Teddiursa Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Teddiursa family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.tera orb: An orb that holds within it the power to crystallize. When it is charged with energy, it can be used to cause Pokémon to Terastallize.
items.timburr sweat: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.tinkatink hair: Tinkatink Hair is a Pokémon material dropped by the Tinkatink family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.tiny bamboo shoot: A small and rare bamboo shoot. It’s quite popular with a certain class of gourmands.
items.toedscool flaps: Toedscool Flaps is a Pokémon material dropped by the Toedscool family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.tofu: An ingredient with a touch of sweetness. It’s unclear how well this raw block of tofu will serve as a sandwich filling.
items.tomato: This very tart ingredient has a lot of savory elements to it, making it popular with many Pokémon.
items.torkoal coal: Torkoal Coal is a Pokémon material dropped by the Torkoal family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.toxel sparks: Toxel Sparks is a Pokémon material dropped by the Toxel family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.tropius leaf: Tropius Leaf is a Pokémon material dropped by the Tropius family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.tynamo slime: Tynamo Slime is a Pokémon material dropped by the Tynamo family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.varoom fume: Varoom Fume is a Pokémon material dropped by the Varoom family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.vee-vee pick: A pick designed after Eevee, sporting the Pokémon’s usual expression. It’s easy to grab by the ears, which is part of why it’s so popular.
items.veluza fillet: Veluza Fillet is a Pokémon material dropped by the Veluza family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.venonat fang: Venonat Fang is a Pokémon material dropped by the Venonat family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.vinegar: A very sour condiment. Vinegars derived from grapes are popular in Paldea. Highly effective when used to add just a light note of flavor.
items.violet book: A record of the expedition that the author, Heath, went on within the Great Crater of Paldea. The book has the name “Turo” written on it in clumsy handwriting.
items.volbeat fluid: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.voltorb sparks: Voltorb Sparks is a Pokémon material dropped by the Voltorb family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.vullaby feather: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.vulpix fur: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.wasabi: This very spicy condiment seems close to horseradish at first, but you’ll find that it has a superb flavor all its own.
items.water tera shard: Water Tera Shard allows a Pokémon's Tera Type to be changed to Water in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet. It can be changed by speaking to the cook in the Treasure Eatery, Medali, and requires 50 Water Tera Shards.
items.watercress: A very bitter ingredient that stands out among veggies for its quirky flavor. People tend to either like it or not like it—they rarely sit on the fence.
items.wattrel feather: Wattrel Feather is a Pokémon material dropped by the Wattrel family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.whimsical tablecloth: A tablecloth made in collaboration with a popular picture-book author. The gently drawn Pokémon make even the loveliest picnic table more charming.
items.whipped cream: A very sweet condiment. Folks in Paldea seem to love the varieties that really let the sweetness sing.
items.white dish: A dish for picnics. Comes in plenty of colors to choose from based on the food or the mood. The white variety comes recommended by sellers.
items.wiglett sand: Wiglett Sand is a Pokémon material dropped by the Wiglett family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.wingull feather: Wingull Feather is a Pokémon material dropped by the Wingull family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.winking pika pick: A Pick-achu, if you will, sporting Pikachu’s winking face. It’s easy to grab by the ears, which is part of why it’s so popular.
items.wooper slime: Wooper Slime is a Pokémon material dropped by the Wooper family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.yanma spike: Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs.
items.yarn ball: A small ball of yarn. It’s woven tight so as not to unravel, but even if it were to come undone, at least you could still use the yarn for something.
items.yellow bell pepper: An ingredient with some bitterness. It’s not much different from its red counterpart in flavor, but mixing the colors makes a feast for the eyes.
items.yellow bottle: A go-to bottle widely used for drinks at picnics. Its simple design and portability make it popular among all sorts of people.
items.yellow cup: A cup sold with the Yellow Bottle. The two don’t stack, so they’re tough to pack—but they look good, so they’re popular with those who like to post picnic photos.
items.yellow dish: A dish for picnics. Comes in plenty of colors to choose from based on the food or the mood. The yellow variety is impossible to go wrong with.
items.yellow tablecloth: A simple yellow tablecloth. It’s smooth and pleasant to the touch, and dishes can be placed on it with hardly a sound.
items.yogurt: A condiment that’s very sweet and very sour. It goes especially well with fruit and is easy to incorporate into a sandwich.
items.yungoos fur: Yungoos Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Yungoos family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.zangoose claw: Zangoose Claw is a Pokémon material dropped by the Zangoose family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.
items.zorua fur: Zorua Fur is a Pokémon material dropped by the Zorua family when they are captured or defeated in battle. It is used to craft TMs in Pokémon Scarlet/Violet using the TM Machine.

moves.896: Blazing Torque deals damage and has a 30% chance of burning the target. Only the Revavroom used by Team Star has this move.
moves.897: Wicked Torque deals damage and has a 10% chance of putting the target to sleep. Only the Revavroom used by Team Star has this move.
moves.898: Noxious Torque deals damage and has a 30% chance of poisoning the target. Only the Revavroom used by Team Star has this move.
moves.899: Combat Torque deals damage and has a 30% chance of paralyzing the target. Only the Revavroom used by Team Star has this move.
moves.900: Magical Torque deals damage and has a 30% chance of confusing the target. Only the Revavroom used by Team Star has this move.

abilities.Cotton Down: When the Pokémon is hit by an attack, it scatters cotton fluff around and lowers the Speed stat of all Pokémon except itself.
abilities.Effect Spore: Has a 30% chance of inflicting either paralysis, poison, or sleep on attacking Pokémon on contact.
